supply_chain.dependencies: Substantially all infrastructure relating to cloud solution outsourced to third-party hosting services (AWS, Azure, GCP). Dependence on third-party cloud infrastructure providers for hosting software.
supply_chain.risks: Disruption in operations of third-party hosting services could negatively affect cloud-based products. Limitation on capacity of third-party hosting services could impede ability to onboard new customers or expand usage. Service agreements termination or lapse could cause interruptions and significant delays. Dependence on third-party internet service providers for connectivity.

regulatory_risks: EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) compliance
Data localization laws and cross-border personal data transfer restrictions
Anti-corruption and anti-bribery laws (FCPA, UK Bribery Act)
U.S. export controls and Export Administration Regulations
Import and export licensing requirements for encryption technology
Federal Trade Commission regulations on AI and consumer protection
Section 404 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act (internal controls)
Tax law changes (IRC Section 174, Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, Inflation Reduction Act)
OECD Pillar Two minimum tax regime (15% global minimum tax)
Sales and use tax collection obligations (post-Wayfair)
Labor regulations in Europe (more advantageous to employees than US)
Brexit-related regulatory changes affecting UK operations

sanction_risks: Sanctions imposed on Russia related to Ukraine war
Potential sanctions affecting operations in embargoed jurisdictions
Export control violations could result in fines, penalties, and incarceration
